docket create \\
  --title "Scaffold SvelteKit app in web/ hidden from user repos" \\
  --desc "Init SvelteKit + shadcn-svelte in web/ inside the docket CLI repo. Add web/node_modules and web/.svelte-kit to .gitignore. App reads .docket/ path from DOCKET_DIR env var \(falls back to cwd\). Vite server-side load functions \(page.server.ts\) read ticket markdown + config.json from disk — no Go changes needed." \\
  --priority 2 \\
  --labels "feature" \\
  --parent TKT-108

docket create \\
  --title "Board view with config-driven columns and ticket cards" \\
  --desc "Kanban board layout. Columns derived from config.json states sorted by column field. Each ticket renders as a Card with title, ID badge, priority, labels, state. Only open-state tickets shown by default. Columns scroll independently. Use shadcn-svelte Card + Badge." \\
  --priority 2 \\
  --labels "feature" \\
  --parent TKT-108

docket create \\
  --title "Ticket detail slide-in panel with markdown rendering" \\
  --desc "Clicking a ticket card opens a Sheet \(shadcn-svelte\) from the right. Renders full ticket markdown: description, AC checklist, comments, handoff notes. Use a lightweight markdown renderer \(e.g. marked or mdsvex\). Sheet closes on Escape or click-outside." \\
  --priority 2 \\
  --labels "feature" \\
  --parent TKT-108

docket create \\
  --title "Filter bar and list view toggle" \\
  --desc "Persistent filter bar above the board: filter by state \(multi-select\), label, priority range. Toggle between board view and a flat list view \(sortable table\). List view is useful when there are many tickets across many columns. Use shadcn-svelte Select, Badge, Tabs or Button group." \\
  --priority 3 \\
  --labels "feature" \\
  --parent TKT-108

docket create \\
  --title "docket ui command to launch dev server" \\
  --desc "Add a docket ui Go subcommand that cd's into web/ \(relative to docket binary location or DOCKET_UI_DIR env\) and runs pnpm dev with DOCKET_DIR set to the current working directory. Prints the local URL on start. Fails gracefully if pnpm or the web/ dir is not found." \\
  --priority 3 \\
  --labels "feature" \\
  --parent TKT-108)

Bash(export DOCKET_ACTOR="agent:claude-sonnet-4-6" && \\
docket create --title "Smarter direct-edit validation: accept valid, reject+educate invalid" \\
  --desc "When docket sync/validate detects direct-edited ticket files, validate each against the full schema. If valid: accept the changes but print a warning showing the equivalent CLI command \(e.g. 'docket update TKT-NNN --state done'\). If invalid: revert the changes, clearly explain the schema violation, and show the CLI command that would achieve what they were trying to do. Never silently drop changes or silently accept schema violations." \\
  --priority 1 && \\
docket create --title "Expand help-json agent_instructions with ticket-writing guide" \\
  --desc "The agent_instructions field in help-json output is read by LLMs before they use docket. Expand it from a single line into a structured guide covering: \(1\) keep tickets atomic - one deliverable, completable in a single focused session; \(2\) description must explain context and constraints, not just the task name; \(3\) every ticket needs at least 2 acceptance criteria that are specific and testable; \(4\) if a task would touch more than 3 files or take multiple logical steps, split it into children under a parent epic; \(5\) cheap/small LLMs should be able to understand and execute the ticket without asking clarifying questions." \\
  --priority 1 && \\
docket create --title "docket create quality validation: enforce description and AC minimums" \\
  --desc "Add validation to docket create: \(1\) require --desc or reject with a helpful message; \(2\) warn if description is under 50 characters - it is probably just restating the title; \(3\) after ticket creation, prompt or remind the user to add acceptance criteria via docket ac add. Optionally support --ac flag on create to add first AC inline. The goal is to make it hard to create low-quality tickets accidentally." \\
  --priority 2 && \\
docket create --title "docket validate quality checks: AC count, desc length, reasoning trail" \\
  --desc "Extend docket validate beyond schema checks to ticket quality: warn on tickets with 0 AC items, descriptions under a word threshold \(e.g. 20 words\), in-progress tickets with no comments \(no reasoning trail\). These are soft warnings, not errors - they do not fail the exit code unless a --strict flag is passed. Helps catch tickets that are too vague for an LLM to execute without guesswork." \\
  --priority 2 && \\
docket create --title "Version update notifications: detect and surface new docket releases" \\
  --desc "Embed a version string at build time via ldflags. On any docket command, check ~/.cache/docket/version_check \(a file with timestamp + latest version\). If the file is older than 24h, fetch the GitHub releases API in the background and update the cache. If the cached latest version is newer than the embedded version, print a one-line notice at the end of output with the upgrade command. The background check must never add latency to the main command." \\
  --priority 3)

Bash(export DOCKET_ACTOR="agent:claude-sonnet-4-6" && docket create --title "docket upgrade — versioned artifact management" --desc "Implement docket upgrade command that updates installed artifacts \(hooks, CLAUDE.md block\) without overwriting user-owned config. Also implement a migration runner for breaking changes between docket versions.

Rules:
- .git/hooks/pre-commit — always overwrite \(docket-owned\)
- CLAUDE.md docket block — replace only between <!-- docket:start --> / <!-- docket:end --> markers
- .docket/config.yaml — never overwrite; only add missing keys with comments
- User content outside docket markers — never touch

Migrations live in src/migrations/ keyed by semver, run in order on upgrade. Each migration is a transform function over .docket/ files.

Depends on TKT-131 \(docket install — git hook infrastructure\)." --priority 1 --labels "feature,hooks" 2>&1)

Bash(export DOCKET_ACTOR="agent:claude-sonnet-4-6" && docket create --title "Runnable verification commands on AC items" --desc "Extend AC items to support an optional run: field containing a shell command. When docket ac check TKT-NNN runs, it executes any run: commands, captures exit codes and stdout, and uses them as pass/fail evidence.

Usage: docket ac add TKT-NNN --desc \\"Tests pass\\" --run \\"pnpm test --coverage\\"

The check command should:
- Run each run: command in the repo root
- Capture exit code and last 20 lines of stdout as evidence
- Auto-populate evidence on pass; mark failed with captured output
- Support --dry-run to show what would be executed without running

This feature is foundational for TKT-D \(smart stack defaults\), TKT-E \(human interactive AC prompt\), and TKT-H \(AC templates\)." --priority 1 --labels "feature,ac-enforcement" 2>&1)

Bash(export DOCKET_ACTOR="agent:claude-sonnet-4-6" && docket create --title "Human interactive AC prompt in pre-commit hook" --desc "When a human runs git commit and the commit references a docket ticket, the pre-commit hook renders the AC list interactively. For each incomplete AC item, it asks the user to confirm completion. Runnable ACs \(TKT-133\) are executed automatically; prose-only ACs get a y/n prompt.

Flow:
1. Hook extracts ticket ID from commit message
2. Fetches AC list via docket ac list TKT-NNN --format json
3. For each incomplete AC with run:: executes and shows pass/fail
4. For each incomplete AC without run:: prompts \\"Did you complete: <desc>? [y/N]\\"
5. Any 'n' or failed run blocks the commit with a clear message and fix suggestion

This is the same logic path as LLM auto-check — just different I/O. Both should call the same underlying library function.

Depends on TKT-131 \(docket install\), TKT-133 \(runnable AC commands\)." --priority 2 --labels "feature,hooks,dx" 2>&1)
Bash(export DOCKET_ACTOR="agent:claude-sonnet-4-6" && docket show TKT-131 --format context 2>&1)

docket comment TKT-131 --body "Refined hook enforcement logic: hook should NOT block commits with no ticket reference \(backward compatible\). It SHOULD block if the current commit message references a ticket that is already in 'done' state. Only the current commit message is inspected — no git history traversal." 2>&1)

The blocking enforcement is captured in AC 6: block only when a referenced ticket is already in 'done' state. These are two distinct behaviors: missing reference = warn and pass; stale/done reference = hard block." 2>&1)
